title: Micropholis ulei
taxonomy: Micropholis ulei is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a type of evergreen tree that is native to the Amazon rainforest. The classification of Micropholis ulei places it within the family Sapotaceae, which includes other tree species such as the sapodilla and the mamey sapote. Micropholis ulei is further classified within the genus Micropholis, a group of trees characterized by their small to medium size and simple, alternate leaves. Micropholis ulei is often classified as a synonym of Micropholis egleri or Micropholis venosa.

intro: Micropholis ulei, also known as Ulei's tree, is a plant species belonging to the Sapotaceae family, native to the Amazonian region of South America. The bark of Micropholis ulei has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Micropholis ulei include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the bark is often used to make infusions, teas, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies such as tinctures and salves.
